title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick — Page 352
raw_text:
[14:59] The ultimate disobedience to the BIP is to refuse to admit that it even
[truly] exists, even though it has the power to torment, humiliate and kill (on a
less than absolute level). This is why it can truly be said that the [yin BIP]
world is an illusion. Hence, it only seemed to kill Christ; in actuality it could
not. Its power is only relative—a seeming. Viz:
 
Stage 1: One does not perceive the BIP, but sees only a mixed
world.
Stage 2: One detects the ruling BIP.
Stage 3: One disobeys it.
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
